description: Data file consumed by gitignore-guard.sh. Declares the minimum patterns that a project's .gitignore should cover when specific stack markers are present. Each rule is ONE required pattern concept; variant spellings of the same pattern are listed under required_patterns with match_any=true (so .env and .env/ satisfy the same rule). Distinct concepts (e.g. __pycache__ vs *.pyc) are declared as SEPARATE rules — this ensures the 'minimum complete set' semantics: every rule must be satisfied for its stack. Hook is informational only, never blocks.

rules.description: Node.js projects must ignore node_modules directory
rules.rationale: node_modules contains thousands of files and should never be committed; accidental staging via git add -A is common

rules.description: Python projects should ignore __pycache__ directories
rules.rationale: Python auto-generates __pycache__ directories on import; committing them causes merge conflicts

rules.description: Python projects should ignore *.pyc bytecode files
rules.rationale: Compiled bytecode files are machine-specific and regenerable; never commit

rules.description: Python projects should ignore virtual environments
rules.rationale: Virtual environments contain thousands of binary files and are machine-specific; never commit

rules.description: Projects with .env files should ignore them
rules.rationale: Committed .env files are the most common source of leaked credentials on public repos

rules.description: Projects with .env files should also ignore local overrides
rules.rationale: Local .env overrides contain per-developer secrets and must never be committed

rules.description: Rust projects must ignore target directory
rules.rationale: Rust build artifacts in target/ can reach gigabytes and are entirely regenerable

rules.description: Go projects should ignore the bin/ directory
rules.rationale: Go compiled binaries are machine-specific and should not be committed

rules.description: Go projects should ignore compiled test binaries
rules.rationale: `go test -c` produces *.test binaries that are machine-specific

rules.description: Maven projects should ignore target/
rules.rationale: Maven output directory is regenerable and bloats the repo

rules.description: Gradle projects should ignore build/
rules.rationale: Gradle output directory is regenerable and bloats the repo

rules.description: Java projects should ignore *.class files
rules.rationale: Compiled .class bytecode is machine-specific and regenerable

rules.description: Gradle projects should ignore .gradle/ cache
rules.rationale: Gradle's local cache contains machine-specific state

rules.description: Projects with JetBrains IDEs should ignore .idea/
rules.rationale: JetBrains config contains absolute paths and user-specific settings

rules.description: Projects with VS Code should consider ignoring .vscode/ (case-by-case)
rules.rationale: VS Code settings may contain per-developer paths; some teams commit shared config instead

rules.description: JetBrains module files should be ignored
rules.rationale: *.iml files contain module paths specific to the developer's machine

rules.description: Projects should ignore macOS metadata files
rules.rationale: macOS Finder creates .DS_Store in every directory it views; no repository value

rules.description: Projects should ignore Windows thumbnail cache
rules.rationale: Windows Explorer generates Thumbs.db automatically; no repository value

rules.description: Projects should ignore Windows desktop.ini files
rules.rationale: Windows creates desktop.ini with per-folder display settings; no repository value

rules.description: Frontend build output should be ignored (dist/)
rules.rationale: Frontend build output is regenerable; committing causes merge conflicts and bloats the repo

rules.description: Frontend build output should be ignored (build/)
rules.rationale: Create-React-App and similar produce build/; not intended for version control

rules.description: Next.js projects should ignore .next/ and out/
rules.rationale: Next.js build cache and server output are regenerable

rules.description: Next.js static export directory should be ignored
rules.rationale: Next.js static export output is regenerable from source

rules.description: Framework last-run artifacts are ephemeral and should be ignored
rules.rationale: Last-run JSON artifacts are per-run outputs and can be regenerated; committing them causes churn
